label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 10

extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won the 31.1km Alicante time trial by 48 seconds over ROGLIČ Primož, extending his overall lead to 2:41.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL averaged 55.7 km/h vs CAVAGNA's 54.4, showing 1.3 km/h advantage reveals sustainable power ceiling difference on pan-flat courses.
ROGLIČ took 12 seconds from CAVAGNA but lost 48 to EVENEPOEL, indicating functional TT ability without true specialist power.
ALMEIDA lost 20+ seconds in team car evacuation lane, suggesting Quick-Step sacrificed support vehicle positioning for leader optimization.
MAS conceded 1+ minute to ROGLIČ, revealing psychological aversion to TT discipline translates to measurable power deficit beyond aerodynamics.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 48-second gap on 31km course indicates 2.6% speed advantage, translating to approximately 60+ watts sustained power superiority
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: 12-second improvement over CAVAGNA benchmark maintains podium viability despite losing time to leader
rider_signals.MAS Enric: negative: Losing 1+ minute to ROGLIČ confirms psychological weakness in TT discipline creates exploitable vulnerability in Grand Tour challenges
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Strong TT performance described as rekindling Giro form suggests late-race climbing form may peak when others fade
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Self-described upset at 1:17 deficit indicates unrealistic expectations or unexpected physical deficit for young rider
rider_signals.RODRÍGUEZ Carlos: neutral: Wearing white jersey as substitute suggests UAE prioritized AYUSO's preparation despite RODRÍGUEZ being viable GC option
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Wrong-turn evacuation lane error cost 20 seconds, revealing concentration lapse under pressure in critical GC moment
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Held hot seat most of day at 54.4 km/h, establishing specialist benchmark that only EVENEPOEL significantly exceeded
